misconceptionSummary: Students express the remainder as a digit rather than showing it as a fraction or decimal of the divisor.

visuals.ruleText: A remainder can be expressed as 'r n', a fraction, or a rounded decimal
visuals.subText: Which form to use depends on the context of the question

steps.narration: When a division leaves a remainder, the result can be expressed in three ways: as a whole-number answer with remainder, as a mixed number with the remainder written as a fraction over the divisor, or as a decimal. The question context determines which form is appropriate.
Four goes into twenty-nine seven times, since four times seven is twenty-eight. The remainder is twenty-nine minus twenty-eight, which is one.

Seven remainder one. As a mixed number: seven and one quarter, because the remainder one is one part out of four. As a decimal: seven point two five, because one divided by four is zero point two five.

visuals.label: Three equivalent forms — context decides which to use

steps.narration: Seven remainder one, seven and one quarter, or seven point two five — all correct. Read the question to know which form is expected.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: writing 29 ÷ 4 = 7 r 1 when asked for a fraction or decimal answer. As a fraction: 7¼. As a decimal: 7.25. The remainder must be expressed relative to the divisor.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: The remainder alone is not a complete answer in all contexts. Express it as a fraction by writing it over the divisor, or continue dividing to get a decimal. Choose the form the question asks for.
